description: Re-anchor the harness from the LiteLLM-removal checkpoint to the follow-up task of refreshing the AICodeWith model catalog and runtime bindings.
steps: Record in `PROGRESS.md` that the user requested a new AICodeWith model set for `/model list` and post-verification restarts of both `gateway` and `serve`.
Replace `PLAN.json` so the active follow-up tracks the model-catalog refresh instead of the already-completed LiteLLM removal.
Preserve the unrelated `tests/test_coding_mode.py` `CodingConfig` import failure as an existing repository caveat during this follow-up.

description: AICodeWith exposes the requested curated model list in `/model list` using the exact user-facing names.
steps: Remove the older `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2`, `claude-*4-5`, and `gemini-2.5-*` AICodeWith catalog entries.
Refresh the README AICodeWith examples and wording to match the new bare model names.

description: Runtime model switching honors the refreshed AICodeWith list without cross-family fallback surprises.
steps: Make `/model <name>` prefer the current available-model binding on exact match so AICodeWith list entries resolve back to `provider: aicodewith`.
Tighten `nanobot/providers/custom_provider.py` so AICodeWith openai-route requests do not silently fall back from `glm`/`deepseek`/`kimi` to legacy GPT models.
Keep Claude and Gemini route detection working with the new bare model names.

description: The refreshed AICodeWith model set is covered by focused tests, live smoke checks, and service restarts.
steps: Update and run the targeted pytest coverage for catalog, command routing, and custom-provider behavior.
Use the configured AICodeWith key to live-check the requested models through the current runtime path.
Restart both `nanobot gateway` and `nanobot serve` after verification and record the evidence in `PROGRESS.md`.
